Github workflows which require explicit permissions
The snippet is visible to any logged in user except external users.
Authored 1 month ago by Ivan Grokhotkov
Workflows needing explicit permissions: — espressif org
After changing the org default GITHUB_TOKEN permission to read-only, these workflows need explicit permissions: blocks because they perform write operations.

Summary
Count
Total workflows scanned	875
Already have permissions:	175
Missing permissions: (no write ops)	358
Need attention	342
Affected repos	114
ESP8266_RTOS_SDK
.github/workflows/issue_comment.yml
Triggers: issue_comment Inferred permissions needed: issues: write

Job	Evidence	Suggested permission
sync_issue_comments_to_jira	uses: espressif/github-actions/sync_issues_to_jira	issues: write
.github/workflows/new_issues.yml
Triggers: issues Inferred permissions needed: issues: write

Job	Evidence	Suggested permission
sync_issues_to_jira	uses: espressif/github-actions/sync_issues_to_jira	issues: write
.github/workflows/new_prs.yml
Triggers: schedule Inferred permissions needed: issues: write

Job	Evidence	Suggested permission
sync_prs_to_jira	uses: espressif/github-actions/sync_issues_to_jira	issues: write

Job	Evidence	Suggested permission
sync_prs_to_jira	uses: espressif/github-actions/sync_issues_to_jira	issues: write

xtensa-isa-doc
.github/workflows/build.yml
Triggers: push, pull_request Inferred permissions needed: contents: write

Job	Evidence	Suggested permission
build-docs	uses: eine/tip	contents: write
How to fix
Add a permissions: block to each workflow or job. Example:

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write
Prefer the least privilege needed. Set permissions at the job level when different jobs need different permissions.
